H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, strong confidence. Exceeds AutoGluon overstim prediction of 6.859 indicating stronger-than-expected efficacy. Qualitative data reports high satisfaction with relaxation effects and balanced rhythm perception. YouTube views highest in dataset (30) but no retention data available. Distribution peaks at 8-9 range with solid central tendency. Multiple analysts confirm this as effective but underutilized protocol requiring better discovery.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Very effective for overstimulation - most users feel noticeably calmer. Works especially well in relaxed settings like driving or just listening.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, strong confidence. Aligns closely with AutoGluon overstim prediction of 6.859. Qualitative feedback shows noticeable improvement with varied settle times. YouTube views very low (5). Distribution centers 6-8 range with expected variance for overstimulation protocol. Consistent across analysts as reliable middle-tier option for overstimulation management.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: A reliable option for overstimulation that helps most users feel better. Results are consistent but not dramatic for most.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, meaningful confidence. Exceeds AutoGluon overstim prediction suggesting underestimation of efficacy. Qualitative data notes effectiveness for sleep preparation despite overstimulation labeling. YouTube views low (8). Distribution slightly right skewed with peak in 7-8 range. Several analysts highlight off-label use case as meaningful adaptation opportunity.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Effective for overstimulation, particularly when users need to sleep. Some discover it works better than intended for sleep preparation.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, strong confidence. Performs below AutoGluon anxiety prediction of 7.838 but remains effective for anxiety regulation. Qualitative data shows consistent moderate to significant improvement with 10-minute settle times. YouTube reach is minimal (7 views) despite solid efficacy, suggesting discovery issues. Distribution shows right skew with minimal low-score tail. Multiple analysts note this as effective but underdiscovered content.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: This anxiety protocol helps most users feel calmer, though some find it takes time to work. It's effective but not widely discovered yet.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, meaningful confidence. Aligns with expected anxiety protocol performance though below AutoGluon prediction. Qualitative feedback indicates mixed but generally positive reception with 5-20 minute settle times. YouTube views extremely low (6) limiting engagement insights. Distribution centers around 6-8 range with no anomalous patterns. Consensus shows reliable but modest efficacy for anxiety reduction.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: A solid choice for anxiety relief with consistent results, though some users notice the music can feel repetitive at times.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, meaningful confidence. Performs consistently across levels despite expected L1-L3 progression. Qualitative data reports noticeable to moderate improvement with ~10 minute settle times. YouTube presence negligible (4 views). Distribution shows moderate spread with slight right skew. Several analysts noted it outperforms L2 despite level progression, warranting investigation into level design consistency.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Works well for anxiety with calming effects that build over time. Some users find it more effective than expected for its level.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, meaningful confidence. Significantly underperforms relative to AutoGluon crisis prediction of 7.955. Qualitative data shows significant improvement reports with quick 5-minute settle times. YouTube views minimal (6). Distribution concentrated in 5-8 range with few extreme scores. Multiple analysts flag this as crisis protocol requiring design revision to meet predicted efficacy.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Helps during crisis moments for many users, though not as strongly as hoped. Works quickly for those who benefit from it.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, meaningful confidence. Performs below AutoGluon crisis prediction of 7.955 despite being top predicted crisis protocol. Qualitative feedback shows moderate to significant improvement with variable settle times. YouTube views extremely low (4). Distribution balanced across 4-8 range. Consensus indicates need for protocol modification to close gap with model expectations.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Provides crisis relief with mixed results - some users find it very helpful while others see more modest benefits.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, meaningful confidence. Underperforms relative to crisis predictions while showing rapid settle times for subset. Qualitative data indicates immediate effects for some users alongside mixed improvement reports. YouTube views very low (5). Distribution shows slight right skew with occasional high scores. Analysts note split between quick efficacy and variable outcomes requiring further study.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Works fast for crisis relief in some cases, but results vary between users. Quick relief possible but not consistent.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, strong confidence. Slightly below AutoGluon sleep prediction of 7.539 but remains strong performer. Qualitative data shows consistent improvement with rapid 5-minute settle times. YouTube views extremely low (4). Distribution shows gentle rise to peak at 9 with tail to 10. Consensus confirms reliable sleep efficacy despite limited discovery metrics.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Helps most users fall asleep easier with consistent results. Works quickly and reliably for sleep preparation.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, meaningful confidence. Performs as expected for sleep protocol though below model prediction. Qualitative feedback includes some 'busy' rhythm perceptions particularly from clinician caregivers. YouTube views very low (5). Distribution tightened around 7-9 range indicating consistent mid-range efficacy. Analysts note potential perspective bias in responses requiring population-aware interpretation.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Generally helpful for sleep, though some users (especially caregivers) find the rhythm too active for rest.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, meaningful confidence. Aligns with expected sleep protocol performance. Qualitative data shows balanced improvement reports with 5-minute settle times predominating. YouTube views negligible (3). Distribution symmetric around mean with minimal extremes. Multiple validators confirm this as dependable sleep aid requiring broader dissemination.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: A dependable sleep aid that works consistently for most users. Effects build gradually but reliably over time.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, meaningful confidence. Significantly underperforms relative to AutoGluon focus prediction of 7.728. Qualitative data reports noticeable improvement with rapid 5-minute settle times. YouTube views very low (5). Distribution spread 3-10 with notable 'busy' rhythm feedback. Agreement exists on need for revision to improve focus efficacy outcomes.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Helps with focus for some users but works less effectively than hoped. Some find the rhythm too active for concentration.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, meaningful confidence. Aligns reasonably with focus protocol expectations. Qualitative feedback shows moderate to significant improvement with 10-minute settle times. YouTube views very low (4). Distribution centered 7-9 range with expected variance. Consensus confirms this as effective focus enhancer requiring better visibility.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: A solid focus enhancer that works well for most users. Effects build steadily and reliably over the session.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, meaningful confidence. Achieves highest observed mean but with elevated variance suggesting heterogeneous response. Qualitative data shows strong significant improvement reports with 10-minute settle times. YouTube views moderate (15) best in THRIVING series. Distribution shows bimodal tendency with cluster at 6-8 and spike at 10. Multiple analysts flag this for subgroup analysis to understand response divergence.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Excellent for focus - many users report major improvements. Results vary between users with some seeing dramatic benefits.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, meaningful confidence. Performs within expected range for creative flow protocol. Qualitative data indicates gradual improvement with split between immediate and 10-minute settle times. YouTube views exceptionally low (2). Distribution centered 6-8 range with right tail to 10. Consistent across reviewers as modest but reliable creative enhancer.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Helps most users think more creatively with modest but reliable improvement. Effects can be immediate or take time to develop.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, meaningful confidence. Shows lowest variance in creative flow cluster indicating consistent response. Qualitative data highlights strong musical appreciation with significant improvement reports. YouTube views exceptionally low (2). Distribution tightly concentrated 6-8 range. Multiple analysts note this as high-consistency option needing better discovery.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Boosts creativity consistently for users who try it. Many find the music especially helpful for creative thinking.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, developing confidence. Exceeds expectations despite developing confidence tier. Qualitative data shows strong improvement reports with 5-10 minute settle times. YouTube views low but better than L1/L2 (7). Distribution skewed high with minimal low scores. Consensus identifies this as promising protocol needing larger sample for confirmation.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Works very well for creative flow when users need it. Strong effects reported though few have tried it so far.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, meaningful confidence. Performs as expected for maintenance protocol with long settle times. Qualitative data shows consistent improvement with 20-minute settle times predominating. YouTube views extremely low (3). Distribution tight around 7-8 range indicating consistent mid-efficacy. Analysts note reliable but slow-acting maintenance option.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Helps maintain balance reliably, though it takes time to work. Best for users who can commit to the full session.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, meaningful confidence. Shows higher variance suggesting differential response by rater perspective. Qualitative data reveals clinician-caregiver split in rhythm perception affecting outcomes. YouTube views very low (4). Distribution spreads 2-10 range indicating heterogeneous response. Consensus highlights need to address perspective bias in interpretation.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Results vary more for this balance protocol - some users find it very helpful while others notice mixed effects.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, developing confidence. Performs near expected levels despite developing tier. Qualitative data includes 'did not settle' reports alongside standard improvement patterns. YouTube views negligible (3). Distribution centered 7-8 range with minimal extremes. Multiple validators note mixed efficacy requiring larger sample for clarification.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Shows promise for balance maintenance but results are inconsistent so far. More testing needed to understand its effectiveness.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, meaningful confidence. Underperforms relative to AutoGluon low zone prediction of 8.230 despite cross-bucket suggestions. Qualitative data shows strong musical appreciation with balanced improvement reports. YouTube views low (8). Distribution moderate spread with central tendency in 6-7 range. Multiple analysts note this as promising but needing efficacy improvement.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Helps with mood and energy for many users but works less effectively than hoped. The music is a strong positive factor.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, meaningful confidence. Performs below low zone predictions despite positive qualitative feedback. Qualitative data highlights significant improvement reports with frequent immediate effects. YouTube views low (8). Distribution tight 6-8 range. Significant embedding drift noted suggesting evolving response patterns requiring monitoring.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Boosts mood and energy consistently though not as strongly as predicted. Many users report quick positive effects.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, developing confidence. Performs below expectations for low zone protocol. Qualitative data shows mixed reports including no effect responses. YouTube views very low (4). Distribution slight left skew with central tendency in 5-6 range. Consensus identifies this as needing revision to improve efficacy for mood and energy regulation.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Provides modest mood and energy help for users who try it. Results are consistent but limited in strength.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, meaningful confidence. Significantly underperforms relative to AutoGluon motivation prediction of 7.876. Qualitative data shows moderate to significant improvement with quick 5-minute settle times. YouTube views low (5). Distribution wide with low-score tail indicating inconsistent response. Multiple analysts flag this for redesign to improve motivation efficacy.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Helps with motivation for some users but works less effectively than hoped. Effects are inconsistent across users.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, developing confidence. Performs below expectations for motivation protocol. Qualitative data shows slight to moderate improvement with variable settle times including 20-minute outliers. YouTube views low (7). Distribution fairly tight 6-7 range suggesting limited efficacy band. Consensus indicates need for revision to enhance motivational impact.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Provides modest motivation help for users who try it. Results are consistent but not strong for most people.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, developing confidence. Shows unexpectedly high mean for L3 with high variance suggesting heterogeneity. Qualitative data confirms moderate improvement with rapid settle times. YouTube views low (7). Distribution extremely wide indicating split response patterns. Notable for significant embedding drift and reverse level efficacy requiring investigation.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Shows unexpected results for motivation - some users benefit greatly while others see little change. Needs investigation.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, meaningful confidence. Underperforms relative to AutoGluon mindclarity prediction of 7.538. Qualitative data shows strong musical appreciation with balanced rhythm perception. YouTube views low (8). Distribution slightly left skewed with central tendency in 6-7 range. Agreement exists on solid but improvable efficacy for mindset shifting.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Helps shift mindset for most users with reliable results. The music works better than the rhythm for many people.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, developing confidence. Underperforms relative to model prediction despite positive qualitative feedback. Qualitative data notes significant improvement reports with occasional immediate effects. YouTube views moderate (12) indicating better discovery. Distribution tight around 6-8 range. Several analysts cite this as promising but needing refinement.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Improves thinking clarity for users who try it. Many report quick effects though the music helps more than rhythm.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, developing confidence. Shows lowest mean and highest variance in dataset suggesting subgroup effects. Qualitative data mixed with limited sample (n=17) showing noticeable improvement but also negative reports. YouTube views moderate (8). Distribution appears bimodal with peaks at low and high scores. Strong consensus across analysts that this requires subgroup investigation before further development.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Shows mixed results so far - some users benefit while others see little change. More testing needed to understand why.

sphere_commentary.overview:
HEALING demonstrates greatest response consistency while all three series show comparable mean efficacy in the 6.5-7.3 range. TRANSFORMING exhibits expected greater variance due to its ascending-arc design for variable affect states. Most protocols perform within expected ranges, though several show notable deviations from model predictions requiring investigation.
• HEALING leads in response consistency with lowest standard deviation across its 12 protocols
• All three series demonstrate comparable mean scores in the 6.5-7.3 range
• TRANSFORMING shows widest distribution as expected from its ascending-arc protocol design
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
HEALING protocols provide most predictable outcomes for anxiety and overstimulation regulation. THRIVING focus and creative flow protocols demonstrate highest peak efficacy with clear L3 progression benefits. TRANSFORMING protocols exhibit greatest response variance, aligning with their design for variable affect states and low-frequency starting points.
• HEALING protocols show most predictable outcomes for anxiety and overstimulation regulation
• THRIVING focus and creative flow protocols demonstrate highest peak efficacy and L3 progression
• TRANSFORMING protocols exhibit greatest response variance, aligning with their design for variable affect states
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
X-MC-L3 reveals unusually low mean and high variance suggesting subgroup effects requiring investigation. The H-CR-L* crisis protocol cluster consistently underperforms relative to model predictions. T-FC-L3 achieves the highest observed mean but with significant score spread indicating heterogeneous response patterns.
• X-MC-L3 shows unusually low mean and high variance warranting subgroup investigation
• H-CR-L1, H-CR-L2, H-CR-L3 all perform below model-predicted crisis efficacy levels
• T-FC-L3 achieves highest mean but with significant score spread suggesting response heterogeneity
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
X-MC-L3 requires subgroup analysis given its low mean and high variance at small sample size. H-CR-L* protocols need modification testing to address consistent underperformance against predictions. T-FC-L3 warrants investigation into its bimodal response distribution. Multiple high-efficacy but low-visibility protocols need improved discovery pathways.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10): Investigate subgroup effects or bimodal response patterns in collected qualitative data
• H-CR-L2 (n=33): Test modified descent arcs or extended hold phases to improve crisis efficacy
• T-FC-L3 (n=35): Analyze response distribution for potential responder subgroups requiring tailored approaches
• H-OS-L1 (n=95) and T-FC-L3 (n=35): Enhance discovery pathways for high-efficacy protocols with low current visibility
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Significant drift observed in multiple protocols suggests evolving response patterns rather than analytical instability. Current dataset lacks sufficient embeddings for cross-protocol clustering analysis. Drift patterns are most pronounced in TRANSFORMING series protocols where sample sizes remain smallest.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: Notable shifts in X-MD-L3 (sim=0.507) and X-ME-L2 (sim=0.532) indicating evolving response patterns
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Insufficient protocol embeddings for cluster analysis in current dataset
• CURRENT CONFIDENCE: Drift patterns likely reflect genuine response evolution rather than analytical instability given increasing sample sizes

by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Provides crisis relief with mixed results - some users find it very helpful while others see more modest benefits.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Works fast for crisis relief in some cases, but results vary between users. Quick relief possible but not consistent.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Very effective for overstimulation - most users feel noticeably calmer. Works especially well in relaxed settings like driving or just listening.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: A reliable option for overstimulation that helps most users feel better. Results are consistent but not dramatic for most.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Effective for overstimulation, particularly when users need to sleep. Some discover it works better than intended for sleep preparation.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Helps most users fall asleep easier with consistent results. Works quickly and reliably for sleep preparation.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Generally helpful for sleep, though some users (especially caregivers) find the rhythm too active for rest.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: A dependable sleep aid that works consistently for most users. Effects build gradually but reliably over time.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Helps most users think more creatively with modest but reliable improvement. Effects can be immediate or take time to develop.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Boosts creativity consistently for users who try it. Many find the music especially helpful for creative thinking.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Works very well for creative flow when users need it. Strong effects reported though few have tried it so far.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Helps with focus for some users but works less effectively than hoped. Some find the rhythm too active for concentration.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: A solid focus enhancer that works well for most users. Effects build steadily and reliably over the session.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Excellent for focus - many users report major improvements. Results vary between users with some seeing dramatic benefits.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Helps maintain balance reliably, though it takes time to work. Best for users who can commit to the full session.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Results vary more for this balance protocol - some users find it very helpful while others notice mixed effects.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Shows promise for balance maintenance but results are inconsistent so far. More testing needed to understand its effectiveness.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Helps shift mindset for most users with reliable results. The music works better than the rhythm for many people.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Improves thinking clarity for users who try it. Many report quick effects though the music helps more than rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Shows mixed results so far - some users benefit while others see little change. More testing needed to understand why.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Helps with motivation for some users but works less effectively than hoped. Effects are inconsistent across users.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Provides modest motivation help for users who try it. Results are consistent but not strong for most people.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Shows unexpected results for motivation - some users benefit greatly while others see little change. Needs investigation.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Helps with mood and energy for many users but works less effectively than hoped. The music is a strong positive factor.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Boosts mood and energy consistently though not as strongly as predicted. Many users report quick positive effects.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Provides modest mood and energy help for users who try it. Results are consistent but limited in strength.
